courses.subject: POPG
courses.title: Global Health

meetings.instructor:
Professor CHUNG Roger Yat Nork, 
Professor DONG Dong, 
Professor TUN Hein Min

Professor CHUNG Roger Yat Nork, 
Professor DONG Dong, 
Professor TUN Hein Min

courses.description: The global nature of public health issues across the domains of practice are of growing importance. Chronic disease problems such as obesity and its consequences as well as communicable diseases such as HIV/AIDS, malaria and TB are of international concern. So too are the health effects of international policies and trade agreements. In addition, public health has a key role to play in disasters such as earthquakes, floods and Tsunami. This course will enable a deeper understanding of a global perspective to non –and communicable disease; the inequalities between nations; the impact of the wider environment on health; response to disasters; the role of international bodies.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Public Health
courses.learning_outcomes:
Student should be able to:
1. Gain awareness of issues in global human health and security
2. Understand major measurements of the health of population and its determinants
3. Have the ability to interpret the reasons for health changes and variations of the world and its nations
4. Obtain skills in accessing, compiling and presenting the health situation of any country
5. Shape an evidence based world view and to be able to engage in debates in global health issues.
courses.course_syllabus:
| **Overview of Global Health: Basic Concepts and Principles** * Definitions of health/global health * Determinants of health * Health and population transitions * Concept of demography and development * Approaches to measuring health |
| --- |
| **Global health Challenges: Disaster and Humanitarian Assistances** * Definition of disasters * Different types of disasters * Health impacts of disasters |
| **Global Health Challenge: Environmental Concerns** * Climate change * Waste-hazards * Indoor/outdoor air-pollution |
| **Global Health Challenge: Non-Communicable Diseases** * Definitions and global trends of NCDs (such as hypertension, diabetics, cancer) * The case of injuries |
| **Global health Challenges:** * Migrants population * Injury |
| Child and Adolescent Health - global and local challenges |
| **Global Health Challenge: Communicable Diseases** * Disease eradication * Neglected tropical diseases * HIV/AIDs & TB * The relationship of nutrition and communicable diseases |
| **Country Development and Health:** * Global public good * Challenges of cross-border collaborations/governance * The global health actors * MDGs & post-2015 agendas |
courses.required_readings:
* Merson MH, Black RE & Mills A (2006) International Public Health: Diseases, Programs, Systems, and Policies. 2nd Edition. Jones and Bartlett Publishers. Sudbury, Massachusetts.

Selected articles of relevant topics will be distributed before classes
courses.recommended_readings:
* Koop CE, Pearson CE & Schwarz MR (2001). Critical Issues in Global Health. Jossey-Bass. A Wiley Company.

The World Bank (2004). The Millennium Development Goals for Health: Rising to the Challenges.

courses.subject: POPG
courses.title: Global Child and Adolescent Health
courses.description: The purpose of this class is to examine child and adolescent health in developed and developing countries. Biomedical, socio-medical, behavioural, economic and political factors affecting child and teen health will be systematically explored. Health services and health education for children and adolescents will also be addressed.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Public Health
courses.learning_outcomes:
Student should be able to:
1. Apply public health principles to child and adolescent health
2. Examine and discuss issues that affect health outcomes on children and teens. Some of these key issues include vaccination, education, breast-feeding, screening for high-risk behaviors, anticipatory guidance
3. Understand and describe provision of health services to children and young people

4. Gain insight into socio-political factors impacting on child and adolescent health
courses.course_syllabus:
| Introduction to Child and Adolescent Public Health, Measuring mortality and morbidity, Social Determinants of Health | |
| --- | --- |
| |
| |
| Global strategies to reduce child mortality from infectious diseases | |
| |
| Adolescent Health (Reproductive health; risk-taking behaviour, addictions, mental health) | |
| |
| Breastfeeding in Hong Kong and globally. (Baby Friendly Hospital Initiative, the International Code of Marketing of Breastmilk Substitutes) | |
| |
| Global Status of Child Injuries | |
| |
| Nutrition, Growth and Obesity | |
| |
| Vaccines, Immunisations and the Global Vaccine Action Plan | |
| |
| Child Rights and Media | |
| |
courses.required_readings:
| Blair, M., Stewart-Brown, S., Waterson, T., & Crowther, R. Child Public Health. Oxford University Press (2003) |
| --- |
| Selected references to be distributed before classes |

courses.description: Disaster and humanitarian responses are crucial elements in public health practices. Humanitarian relief actions often highlight the importance of mitigation, preparedness, training and multidisciplinary collaborative response within and beyond the health and medical sector. This course offers an overview of the disaster management cycle and the public health and medical implications of disaster and humanitarian crisis. It aims to show how public health principles may be applied in disaster relief and how evidence-based health related humanitarian actions can be planned, implemented and evaluated.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Public Health
courses.course_syllabus:
| Public health & Humanitarian Principles in Disaster Settings |
| --- |
| Management of Natural and Man-made Hazards & Complex Emergencies |
| Lessons from Great East Japan Earthquake -Challenge in Addressing Complexity of Fukushima |
| SPHERE Standard & Case discussion (1)- The Rohingya refugee crisis in Bangladesh / Myanmar |
| 21st Century Challenge in Disaster and Humanitarian System |
| Responding to Medical and Health Needs in Disaster |
| Preparation of Simulation Exercise |
| Humanitarian Crisis: Simulation Exercise |
| International Policy in Risk Reduction |
| Case discussion (2) Fukushima Daiichi: The Path to Nuclear Meltdown |

courses.subject: POPG
courses.title: Global Health Policy
courses.description: This course aims to provide an overview of global health policy, the area of public health concerned with decisions of policies that translate empirical and epidemiological evidence and political will into consensus and interventions in a global scale. Throughout the course, topics of global health policy will also be explored using the lens of public health ethics and justice.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For TPg students under School of Public Health
courses.course_syllabus:
| Introduction to Global Health Policy and Justice |
| --- |
| Poverty, Development, and Health Equity |
| Health Demography: Urbanization and Migration |
| Global Health Financing: Multi-lateral Foreign Aid In-class Quiz |
| Globalization and Food Security |
| Global Health Policy for Rare and Neglected Diseases |
| Global Pandemic Policies In-class Quiz |
| Group Presentation |
courses.recommended_readings: Brown GW, Yamey G, Wamala S, eds. *The handbook of global health policy*. Wiley Blackwell, UK; 2014.
